title: The Justice Game
rationale: A landmark legal memoir recounting Robertson's most significant cases from the 1970s-1990s, showcasing courtroom advocacy, miscarriages of justice, and the gap between law and justice. Includes the Oz trial, Matrix Churchill, Gay News blasphemy, Rushdie defence, and Antigua investigations. Selected as the root book for legal-practice-and-career as it provides narrative-driven insight into the real practice of law, judicial conduct, and the adversarial system.
